Home

Notes from a long walk

The team gathered before dawn to begin the long ascent up the mountain trail. The weather was kind and visibility was good for the first three hours of the climb.

The descent in the afternoon was the trickier part, on loose scree that demanded careful footing. The group moved slowly and deliberately.

By midday they had reached the col, where they paused for a quick lunch in the lee of a granite outcrop. The wind was steady but not punishing.

From the col the route took them along the ridge, with views opening out across the valley to the south and the smaller summits beyond.

The local guide service confirmed that conditions higher up the mountain were already changing, with the first dusting of snow expected by the weekend.
